Vessels Flying “Flags of Convenience” Controlled Global Alaskan Pollock Supply

Some countries sell the ability to fly their flag to other nations, a tactic used to skirt more stringent fishing regulation.

Pollock sourced from Alaska, an important global stock, travelled thousands of miles on ships from all over the world.

UNITED KINGDOM (8)

PORTUGAL (37)

JAPAN (12)

MALTA (79)

CYPRUS (17)

PANAMA (139)

SINGAPORE (92)

MARSHALL ISLANDS (15)

LIBERIA (265)

Shipments

100

10

1
